packet_label: ThirdPerson human inputs on 2026-04-19 local time
explicit_request: Analyze the April 19, 2026 ThirdPerson packet for recurring needs around default-lane regression proof, runtime animation defects, and durable task-state alignment.

candidate_needs.need: Keep regression proof on the human default lane instead of off-lane automation or proof-view substitutes.
Diagnose and fix the runtime character-readability defect: invisible pawn first, then floating, rounded, or offset feet and rocking/tilt.
Keep pass and bug artifacts synchronized so the human does not have to restate the same constraint or defect framing.

suffering_signals: Explicit complaints about wasted time and missed regression proof.
Escalation from invisible pawn to feet grounding and animation quality.
Repeated insistence on the human default lane only.
Rejection of non-runtime evidence as closure.
highest_leverage_context_to_fetch: Current PASS-0009 runtime proof artifact on the human default lane.
BUG-0004 / HANDOFF / TASK-STATE entries that track the reopened defect and pass framing.
The exact runtime screenshot or video the human is using to judge animation and grounding.

clarifying_question.why_not_needed: The packet already repeats the lane, defect class, and closure bar enough times to avoid burdening the human with another question.

risk_notes: Tone is sharp, but the actionable signal is the default-lane/runtime-evidence mismatch.
Some messages are planning-gate updates rather than runtime observations, so do not collapse them into one cosmetic issue.
Source/export and other non-runtime evidence were explicitly rejected as closure evidence.
